summary: Completed comprehensive testing of multi-tenant data isolation fixes. Found critical data isolation bug where existing users lack restaurant_id association, allowing cross-restaurant data access. Order creation fails due to MongoDB conflict. Frontend UI working well with minor WebSocket issues.

critical_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: Existing users (admin@restaurant.com, kitchen@restaurant.com, billing@restaurant.com) have restaurant_id: null, allowing them to see ALL restaurant orders instead of only their own
critical_bugs.impact: Complete data isolation failure - Restaurant A staff can see Restaurant B orders

critical_bugs.issue: Order creation fails with MongoDB WriteError: 'Updating the path total_orders would create a conflict at total_orders'
critical_bugs.impact: Customers cannot place orders - 500 internal server error

flaky_endpoints.issue: Table creation expects integer table_number but validation error suggests string input

integration_issues.issue: WebSocket connection fails: 'WebSocket is closed before the connection is established'
Order placement fails silently in UI but shows 500 error in console

passed_tests: Super Admin Login and Dashboard (12 restaurants, ₹25,791 MRR)
Restaurant Admin Login and Dashboard (₹540 revenue, 2 orders)
Kitchen Staff Login and Dashboard (proper order columns: Pending, Accepted, Preparing, Ready)
Billing Staff Login and Dashboard (Active Orders: 0, Completed: 2)
Customer Menu Display (11 items, 7 categories, proper ₹ pricing)
Customer Cart Functionality (add items, quantity management, total calculation)
Menu Isolation (Restaurant A menu shows only Restaurant A items/categories)
Analytics Isolation (each restaurant has separate analytics endpoints)
Staff Creation (both restaurants can create kitchen/billing staff)
Restaurant Creation (super admin can create multiple restaurants)
Authentication Flow (all user types can login successfully)

success_percentage.frontend: 85% (UI working well, order placement fails due to backend)

action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix restaurant_id association for existing users (admin@restaurant.com, kitchen@restaurant.com, billing@restaurant.com) - they currently have restaurant_id: null allowing cross-restaurant data access. Also fix MongoDB conflict in order creation (customers.total_orders field update conflict).
